justification: All pass criteria met: (1) /health stub identified as always returning 200 with no dependency checks — called 'a lie' in escalations. (2) Consequence clearly named — load balancers and orchestrators will route traffic to broken instances, silently accepting and failing every request. (3) Specific dependency checks recommended: DB connectivity, SendGrid reachability, queue consumer status, with separate readiness/liveness probes. (4) Circuit breaker absence on SendGrid flagged as 'single highest-priority gap' with detailed resource exhaustion math. (5) Verdict is NOT READY. (6) Logging coverage rated 'adequate' and metrics baseline acknowledged as 'solid start'.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the health check issue — distinguished readiness vs liveness probes, explained exactly how a stub health check harms operations, and correctly classified it as blocking
Circuit breaker analysis includes concrete math (3 retries × 10s = 30s resource burn per request) and cascading failure explanation
Goes beyond rubric requirements with valuable findings: idempotency/duplicate send risk, dead-letter queue gap, poison message handling, missing jitter on retries
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and actionable reasoning
Confidence section is well-calibrated — correctly identifies assumptions and low-confidence areas where spec details were missing
Logging rated 'adequate' but then lists gaps — could be clearer that baseline logging is adequate while noting specific improvements; minor inconsistency
